title: Deploying to Mobile Devices
concept: Mobile deployment requires converting models to platform-specific formats like TensorFlow Lite (TFLite) or CoreML to run on device accelerators.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is knowledge distillation in machine learning?

options.text: Training a small model to mimic the outputs of a large, pre-trained model
Filtering out bad data samples from training sets
Compiling a model for a specific GPU architecture

exercises.explain: Distillation transfers knowledge from a large, complex model (teacher) to a smaller, faster model (student).

cards.title: Speaking the local tongue
cards.body: An Apple iPhone has a Neural Engine, and an Android phone has a Snapdragon NPU. They don't run PyTorch Python scripts directly. To use these hardware accelerators, we must convert our models to CoreML (Apple) or TFLite (Android) formats.

cards.title: Mobile Runtimes & Formats
cards.body: Mobile runtimes execute optimized model files on mobile OS frameworks. CoreML is Apple's framework, optimized for iOS hardware. TensorFlow Lite (TFLite) is Google's cross-platform framework, heavily used on Android and IoT devices.

cards.title: Exporting to Kindle vs. EPUB
cards.body: Writing a model in PyTorch is like writing a manuscript. To read it on an Amazon Kindle, you export it to Kindle format (.mobi). To read it on an iPad, you export it to .epub. The text is the same, but the formatting is optimized for the reader.

cards.title: Real-time AR filters
cards.body: A social media app converts their face-tracking mesh model to CoreML using Apple's coremltools. The model runs on the iPhone Neural Engine, consuming 5x less battery than a raw CPU implementation.

cards.title: Runtimes don't support all operations
cards.body: You might think that converting to TFLite or CoreML is guaranteed to work for any network. In reality, some advanced neural operations or custom activation functions are not supported by mobile compilers, requiring fallback execution on the slow CPU.

cards.title: Mobile AI Hardware Stack
cards.body: How models interface with mobile hardware:

left.text:
File format: .mlmodel / .mlpackage
Runs on Apple Neural Engine (ANE)
iOS-native integration
Optimized for Apple hardware

right.text:
File format: .tflite
Runs on Android NPUs (via NNAPI)
Cross-platform support
Wide support on IoT / Raspberry Pi

exercises.prompt: Which mobile framework is native to Apple iOS devices for running optimized machine learning models?
exercises.explain: CoreML is Apple's native framework designed for high-performance execution on iOS, macOS, and watchOS.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Using a mobile GPU or Neural Processor (NPU) helps preserve battery life compared to running models on the mobile CPU.
exercises.explain: True. Mobile NPUs are designed to run tensor math with extreme energy efficiency, reducing battery drain.

exercises.sentence: To deploy a model to an Android phone, you typically convert it to the ___ format.
exercises.explain: TensorFlow Lite (.tflite) is the standard format for deploying models to Android devices.

exercises.prompt: What happens if a mobile model contains an operation not supported by the phone's NPU?

options.text: The runtime falls back to running that operation on the CPU
The phone will immediately crash or reboot
The model will skip that layer and output random guesses

exercises.explain: Mobile frameworks support CPU fallback, running unsupported nodes on the general CPU, though this slows down inference.

exercises.prompt: You are deploying a hand-gesture tracker on an iOS app. The model needs to run at 60 FPS. Which implementation path should you choose?

options.text: Convert the model to CoreML and configure it to run on the Apple Neural Engine (ANE).
Run a standard Python script on a local web server hosted inside the app container.
Deploy a raw TensorFlow model using CPU fallback on the mobile CPU.
Use cloud API calls for every video frame over cellular networks.

exercises.explain: Next up: Computer Vision in Industry. Converting the model to CoreML and targeting the Neural Engine (ANE) provides the low latency and energy efficiency required for high-framerate real-time tracking on mobile devices.
